original_hypothesis.title: SignalService is intentionally unpausable, preventing emergency shutdown during proof-verification incidents
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: SignalService inherits pause/unpause but overrides _authorizePause to always revert, making it impossible to pause even by owner. If a vulnerability is discovered in proof verification or chain data caching, operators cannot halt proving/verification flows at the contract level, potentially increasing exploit blast radius.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is an operational resilience gap rather than a direct exploit. It becomes critical during incidents involving proof logic or root poisoning, where fast pause is standard containment.
preconditions: An active exploit or critical bug exists in SignalService proof path or caching logic
Operators need on-chain pause for containment
No alternative gating exists (e.g., upstream Bridge pause suffices only partially)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Emergency control missing for a core verification dependency

operational_questions: Is pausing the Bridge sufficient to contain SignalService-related exploits (e.g., third-party apps calling proveSignalReceived directly)?
Do operators have alternative kill-switches (address manager reroute, governance actions)?
What is the incident runbook if SignalService is compromised?
validation_requirements: Model incident where SignalService proof is bypassed and determine containment steps
Verify whether any critical flows bypass Bridge and call SignalService directly

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Exploit a hypothetical proof/caching vulnerability in SignalService
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Continue exploitation while operators attempt to pause
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Pause fails; attackers maintain ability to call proveSignalReceived and/or benefit from poisoned cache
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Escalate exploit impact until address-manager/governance intervention
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Depends on existence of a SignalService bug

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Delayed containment; extended loss window if SignalService bug exists
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: All proof-based systems relying on SignalService

execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: Only during a SignalService vulnerability event

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: None specific absent a bug; monitor unusual proveSignalReceived usage spikes
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Unexpected ChainDataSynced bursts from public proveSignalReceived calls (if caching options used)
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Attackers repeatedly invoking proof verification to manipulate caches or load

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
